meta.note: subscription auth; codex reports tokens, not USD

A.missing: It replaces legal or testamentary entitlement to another's estate with the person's ability to think of it as theirs after death.
A.reason: It does not state that the person is entitled to inherit anything.

B.missing: It replaces legal or testamentary entitlement to an estate with things changing location around a death and a possible claim of ownership.
B.reason: The estate and the legal basis of inheritance are absent.

C.missing: It replaces legal or testamentary entitlement with a reported transfer or movement of a thing after someone dies.
C.reason: It sketches post-death succession but does not encode entitlement by law or will.

D.missing: It replaces legal or testamentary entitlement with someone's belief that some things will be theirs when another dies.
D.reason: An expectation of ownership is neither inheritance nor a legal entitlement.

E.missing: It replaces legal or testamentary entitlement with a person's possible ability to do something with a thing after a death.
E.reason: It does not identify an estate, inheritance, or any entitlement.

F.missing: It replaces legal or testamentary entitlement with an unspecified thing moving to someone after another dies.
F.reason: A post-death transfer can occur for many reasons and does not establish heirship.

G.missing: It replaces legal or testamentary entitlement to inherit another's estate with actual ownership of many things after that person's death.
G.reason: It captures a possible outcome of inheritance but omits the defining legal or will-based entitlement.
